== obdfilter-survey test 1a: Object Storage Targets survey ========================================================== 01:26:01 (1704781561)
+ NETTYPE=tcp thrlo=2 nobjhi=1 thrhi=4 size=1024 case=disk rslt_loc=/tmp targets="192.168.204.137:lustre-OST0000 192.168.204.137:lustre-OST0001" /usr/bin/obdfilter-survey

Patch Set 1:

> bash: lctl: command not found
 > /usr/bin/obdfilter-survey: line 242: ( << 16) | ( << 8) | :
 >   syntax error: operand expected (error token is "<< 16) | ( << 8) | ")
 > /usr/bin/obdfilter-survey: line 254: [: -lt: unary operator expected
 > bash: lctl: command not found
 > bash: lctl: command not found

This looks like it is returning back to the original LU-16861 error in Janitor testing.  This is coming from commands in the obdfilter-survey script itself:

 # inspired from the lustre_build_version routine in test-framework.sh
get_lustre_version() {
        local host=${1:-${unique_hosts[0]}}
        local ver

        ver=$(remote_shell $host "$lctl get_param -n version 2>/dev/null ||
                                $lctl lustre_build_version 2>/dev/null ||
                                $lctl --version 2>/dev/null | cut -d' ' -f2")
        local lver=$(egrep -i "lustre: |version: " <<<$ver | head -n 1)
        [ -n "$lver" ] && ver="$lver"

        sed -e 's/.*: //' -e 's/^v//' -e 's/-.*//' -e 's/_/./g' <<<$ver |
                cut -d. -f1-4
 }

It may be related to patch https://review.whamcloud.com/53083 "LU-16827 obdfilter: Fix 'emfperf obdfilter-survey' error" causing problems with the "$lctl" setting:

 if [ -z "$lustre_root" ]; then       
        lctl=${LCTL:-lctl}
 else                                                                       
        lctl=${lustre_root}/utils/lctl                                   
 fi

I don't think we really need this version check for 2.3 servers anymore, so that could be removed.  However, if there is something wrong with the $lctl variable then the test would just fail later in the script.

Let me update this patch to see if I can see what is wrong.

Strangely, this looks like it is failing with the version code in the old obdfilter-survey script before I deleted it in this patch:

 bash: lctl: command not found
 /usr/bin/obdfilter-survey: line 242: ( << 16) | ( << 8) | :
   syntax error: operand expected (error token is "<< 16) | ( << 8) | ")
 /usr/bin/obdfilter-survey: line 254: [: -lt: unary operator expected
 bash: lctl: command not found
 bash: lctl: command not found

It looks like it is running a previously installed version of the script in /usr/bin?  I've added more debugging to the script to see what is happening.

parents.subject: LU-17054 lnet: use GFP_KERNEL for alloc w/o spinlock

commit.subject: LU-16861 obdfilter: Exclude quotes when getting NIDs
commit.message:
LU-16861 obdfilter: Exclude quotes when getting NIDs

In get_targets(), when getting NIDs the quotes were also included.
Exclude quotes when generating NIDs as they are not required.

Use $LCTL instead of $lctl, and make it also work in Janitor testing.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=obdfilter-survey
